meta.title: Indian Jobs Map
meta.source_secondary: NASSCOM 2024, NITI Aayog Gig Report 2022, QES Q3 2024, Labour Bureau
meta.note: Sample data uses PLFS 2023-24 estimates. Run pipeline/ to regenerate from unit-level microdata.
meta.caveat: AI Exposure does not predict job loss. It estimates the degree to which current AI (language models, vision, code) can reshape daily work — not whether demand for the occupation will fall. An agricultural labourer scoring 1 and a data-entry clerk scoring 9 face very different futures shaped by forces beyond AI alone.

occupations.name: Senior Govt Officials & IAS/IPS
occupations.india_note: Coveted 'sarkari naukri' — IAS, IPS, IFS officers. ~900 IAS vacancies in 2024. Pension, housing, power attract millions of UPSC aspirants annually.
occupations.ai_rationale: Policy drafting and file-noting increasingly AI-assisted, but authority, discretion, and field administration remain deeply human.

occupations.name: Corporate & Business Managers
occupations.india_note: Concentrated in Mumbai, Bengaluru, Delhi NCR, Hyderabad. Growth driven by startups and MNC India operations. Management education boom — 6,500+ MBA colleges.
occupations.ai_rationale: Strategic decisions need human judgment, but reports, presentations, and data analysis are rapidly AI-augmentable.

occupations.name: Production & Operations Managers
occupations.india_note: Manufacturing sector revival under PLI scheme driving demand. Automobile, electronics, pharma clusters in Pune, Chennai, Ahmedabad, Noida.
occupations.ai_rationale: Factory floor supervision requires physical presence; AI assists in scheduling, quality control, and demand forecasting.

occupations.name: Engineers (Civil, Mechanical, Electrical)
occupations.india_note: ~1.5M engineering graduates annually but ~40% face skill-mismatch. Infrastructure push under PM Gati Shakti and National Infrastructure Pipeline driving civil engineering demand.
occupations.ai_rationale: AutoCAD, simulation, structural analysis, and documentation are heavily AI-augmentable. Site supervision remains manual.

occupations.name: Doctors & Medical Specialists
occupations.india_note: India has 1 doctor per 854 people — WHO recommends 1 per 1,000. ~70% of doctors in urban areas serve 30% of population. Medical tourism generates $9B. MBBS seats doubled since 2014.
occupations.ai_rationale: AI radiology, drug discovery, and diagnostic support are real — but patient examination, surgery, and clinical judgment remain irreplaceable.

occupations.name: Nurses & Midwives
occupations.india_note: India produces ~200K nurses annually. Major exporter to Gulf, UK, Canada — creating domestic shortages. Nurse-to-population ratio of 1.7 per 1,000 vs WHO target of 3.
occupations.ai_rationale: Patient care is physical and relational. AI monitors vitals and flags anomalies, but bedside care and emotional support are irreplaceably human.

occupations.name: Primary & Secondary School Teachers
occupations.india_note: ~9.5 lakh govt schools, 3.3 lakh private. 1.1M teacher vacancies as of 2023. NEP 2020 pushing competency-based learning. Private tuition (coaching) industry at ₹5.8L crore separately.
occupations.ai_rationale: Lesson planning and assessment AI-augmentable, but classroom relationships, motivation, and adaptive teaching remain deeply human — especially in rural India.

occupations.name: Software & IT Professionals
occupations.india_note: India's 53L tech workers generate $245B in exports. Second-largest tech workforce globally. Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, Chennai are global delivery hubs. 1.7L H1-B visa holders in USA are Indian IT professionals.
occupations.ai_rationale: Code generation, testing, documentation, and design are being reshaped by AI faster than any other sector. The work is not disappearing — it is being amplified and redefined.

occupations.name: Finance & Accountants (CA, CFA)
occupations.india_note: ~3.4L chartered accountants, 1.5L CFPs. ICAI is world's second-largest accounting body. GST compliance and SEBI regulations driving sustained demand. Big 4 firms expanding India delivery centres.
occupations.ai_rationale: Audit, tax filing, financial modelling — AI tools are advancing fast. But interpretation, client advisory, and regulatory judgment stay human.

occupations.name: University & College Professors
occupations.india_note: India has 1,043 universities and 42,000+ colleges — world's largest higher education system by institution count. 30% faculty posts vacant at central universities.
occupations.ai_rationale: Research, grading, and literature review benefit from AI; teaching, mentorship, and original thinking remain human-centred.

occupations.name: ICT Technicians & IT Support
occupations.india_note: BPO/ITES sector employs ~1.5M in IT support. Rapid shift to AI-assisted helpdesks is already underway. Tier-2 cities (Coimbatore, Indore, Jaipur) becoming new IT hubs.
occupations.ai_rationale: L1/L2 support increasingly handled by AI chatbots. Complex infrastructure work and client-facing roles remain, but volume-based support jobs are being restructured.

occupations.name: Medical & Diagnostic Technicians
occupations.india_note: Diagnostic labs like Lal PathLabs, Dr Lal growing rapidly. AI-powered radiology (Niramai, Qure.ai) already deployed. 900M Ayushman Bharat beneficiaries driving lab testing demand.
occupations.ai_rationale: AI radiology assistants (Qure.ai) already deployed in India. Routine diagnostic work is being augmented, but complex cases and equipment operation stay manual.

occupations.name: Insurance & Finance Agents
occupations.india_note: LIC has 13.5L agents — largest insurance salesforce globally. IRDAI regulations + Jan Suraksha schemes expanding insurance penetration from 4% to target 10% of GDP. Paytm, Policybazaar digitising but agent networks persist.
occupations.ai_rationale: Claims processing and underwriting AI-augmentable; trust-based rural insurance selling requires human agents for years yet.

occupations.name: Office & Admin Clerks
occupations.india_note: Largest SSC (Staff Selection Commission) exam category — 4 crore applicants for 20,000 posts in 2023. Govt office automation under Digital India slowly reducing headcount in new hiring.
occupations.ai_rationale: File management, correspondence, and scheduling — AI handles most of this. India's clerical workforce remains large due to slow digitisation of legacy government workflows.

occupations.name: Data Entry & BPO Operators
occupations.india_note: India's BPO industry employs ~1.4M in voice/non-voice processes. Automation wave already reducing new hiring in transactional BPO. Top firms (WNS, Genpact, EXL) shifting to analytics and AI.
occupations.ai_rationale: Structured data entry is the archetype of work AI and OCR replaces at scale. Already happening — NASSCOM reports 15% headcount reduction in traditional BPO in 2023-24.

occupations.name: Bank Tellers & Cashiers
occupations.india_note: UPI processed 18,000+ crore transactions in 2024 — world's largest real-time payment system. ATMs and UPI/PhonePe/GPay reducing teller transactions by ~30% since 2019.
occupations.ai_rationale: Cash handling and basic transactions increasingly self-served via UPI and ATMs. Relationship banking and loan processing roles remain human.

occupations.name: Cooks & Dhaba/Hotel Staff
occupations.india_note: Dhaba culture, wedding catering, school mid-day meal scheme (12 crore students) drive massive cook employment. Zomato/Swiggy cloud kitchen segment growing 25% YoY creating new format.
occupations.ai_rationale: Cooking requires tactile skill, heat sense, and real-time adaptation. AI has no meaningful current path to replace a tandoor cook or a hotel chef.

occupations.name: Security Guards & Watchmen
occupations.india_note: India has 9M+ private security guards — largest private security industry in the world (CAPSI 2024). Minimum wage ₹12,000–17,000/month by state. CCTV+AI surveillance is expanding, but human presence mandated by law in most settings.
occupations.ai_rationale: Physical deterrence, emergency response, and access control require human presence. AI surveillance assists but cannot legally or practically replace guards at most posts.

occupations.name: Kirana & Shop Owners
occupations.india_note: 1.2 crore kirana stores — India's largest retail network. Reliance JioMart, Amazon Bazaar digitising kiranas via ONDC. UPI adoption in 90%+ of kiranas but bookkeeping still largely manual (Khatabook serves 2Cr businesses).
occupations.ai_rationale: Inventory AI (Khatabook, OkCredit) and UPI billing emerging, but personal relationships and credit extension to known customers are still the kirana's core advantage.

occupations.name: Retail Salespersons & Sales Staff
occupations.india_note: Organised retail (Reliance, DMart, Zudio, Meesho) growing 15% annually. But 93% of India's ₹100L crore retail is still unorganised. Mall culture concentrated in top-8 metros.
occupations.ai_rationale: Product recommendation AI present in organised retail apps; in-store personal selling in jewellery, textiles, electronics remains relationship-based.

occupations.name: Gig Delivery & Platform Workers
occupations.india_note: ~1 crore active gig workers on Swiggy, Zomato, Blinkit, Amazon, Ola, Uber, Zepto, Urban Company. NITI Aayog projects 2.35 crore gig workers by 2029-30. Social security code 2020 recognises gig workers but implementation pending.
occupations.ai_rationale: Route optimisation AI runs the dispatch — but the physical delivery and 10-minute guarantee require human riders. Drone delivery is a decade away at this scale.

occupations.name: Childcare & Anganwadi Workers
occupations.india_note: 14 lakh Anganwadi centres employ 13L workers + 13L helpers under ICDS scheme. Paid ₹4,500–10,000/month — below minimum wage. PM POSHAN (mid-day meal) scheme employs additional 25L cooks.
occupations.ai_rationale: Early childhood development, nutrition monitoring, and community mobilisation require trusted local women workers — irreplaceable by technology.

occupations.name: Urban Company & Home Service Workers
occupations.india_note: Urban Company, Justdial, NoBroker, Housejoy platform for plumbers, AC technicians, beauticians. ~25L workers in platform home services. Monthly earnings ₹15,000–35,000 — significantly above informal peers.
occupations.ai_rationale: Job matching and scheduling is AI-driven on platforms. Actual service delivery (AC repair, beauty, plumbing) remains entirely manual.

occupations.name: Crop Farmers (Self-employed)
occupations.india_note: 86% of India's 14.6 crore farmers own less than 2 hectares. PM-KISAN supports 11 crore farmers with ₹6,000/yr. MSP covers 23 crops but only 6% of farmers sell at MSP. Average farm income: ₹10,218/month (NABARD NAFIS 2023).
occupations.ai_rationale: Precision agriculture apps exist (Cropin, Fasal, DeHaat) but smartphone penetration in small farmers is limited and core work is physical and weather-dependent.

occupations.name: Animal Farmers & Dairy Workers
occupations.india_note: Amul cooperative model covers 36L dairy farmers. India is world's largest milk producer at 230MT/year. NDDB, state cooperatives provide structured income but 75% still sell through informal channels.
occupations.ai_rationale: Animal husbandry is physical and relational. AI-based disease detection apps emerging but adoption minimal at small farmer level.

occupations.name: Fishers & Aquaculture Workers
occupations.india_note: India is world's 3rd largest fish producer. PM Matsya Sampada Yojana investing ₹20,050 crore in fisheries. Deep sea fishing and fish farming under formalization push. Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal, Kerala, Gujarat largest hubs.
occupations.ai_rationale: Deep-sea navigation AI exists (SmartFish) but most Indian fishers rely on traditional knowledge. Core work remains physical and ocean-dependent.

occupations.name: Miners & Stone Quarry Workers
occupations.india_note: Jharkhand, Odisha, Chhattisgarh mineral belt employs millions. Illegal sand mining employs large informal workforce. PMKK scheme training miners for formal sector transition.
occupations.ai_rationale: Underground and quarry work is hazardous and physical. AI used in safety monitoring at large mines but does not reduce labour needs.

occupations.name: Masons & Building Construction Workers
occupations.india_note: PM Awas Yojana (Urban + Gramin) targeting 2.95 crore houses. Smart Cities mission and RERA driving construction boom. Skilled mason shortage is critical — ITI mason enrollments growing 40% since 2020.
occupations.ai_rationale: Masonry, tiling, plastering — these require physical skill in unstructured environments. No credible automation path in India's cost structure.

occupations.name: Electricians & Wiremen
occupations.india_note: Saubhagya scheme electrified 2.86 crore households 2017-2019. Solar rooftop and EV charging infrastructure creating new demand. Urban Company, Sulekha platforms formalising electrical services.
occupations.ai_rationale: Fault diagnosis tools emerging; but wiring in old Indian buildings, mixed standards, and physical installation are manual. Demand growing faster than supply.

occupations.name: Electronics & Mobile Repair Technicians
occupations.india_note: India's mobile repair market — 150 crore active connections, 85 crore smartphones. Every town has multi-floor mobile markets (Palika Bazaar, SP Road). PMKVY providing formal electronics training.
occupations.ai_rationale: Diagnostics AI emerging; physical board-level repair requires manual skill that is India's cost advantage globally.

occupations.name: Textile & Garment Workers
occupations.india_note: Second-largest employer after agriculture. Tiruppur (₹80,000 crore knitwear), Surat (polyester), Bhiwandi (looms), Ludhiana (hosiery). PLI scheme for textiles attracting ₹10,683 crore investment. Competing with Bangladesh, Vietnam for global orders.
occupations.ai_rationale: Garment stitching requires dexterous manual labour. India's wage competitiveness means automation investment is economically unattractive at current scales.

occupations.name: Handicraft & Artisan Workers
occupations.india_note: 7 crore artisans across pottery, woodwork, block printing, embroidery, metalwork. PM Vishwakarma Yojana (2023) provides ₹15,000 toolkit + ₹1-2L collateral-free credit. Meesho, Amazon Karigar digitising craftsperson sales.
occupations.ai_rationale: Handcraft is the antithesis of automation — its value lies in human creation. AI generative art is a market threat, not a workflow replacement.

occupations.name: Auto Rickshaw & Taxi Drivers
occupations.india_note: ~1.5 crore Ola/Uber/Rapido registered drivers. Majority own their vehicles (asset-heavy gig model). EV three-wheelers (Mahindra Treo, Bajaj RE) displacing petrol autos at 15% rate. Mumbai alone has 2L auto-rickshaws.
occupations.ai_rationale: Dispatch fully AI-optimised on platforms. Autonomous vehicles in India's traffic conditions are decades away — the driving skill remains entirely human.

occupations.name: Truck & Bus Drivers
occupations.india_note: ~1 crore truck drivers move ₹180L crore of goods. 7L driver shortage estimated in 2024. PM e-Bus Sewa deploying 10,000 e-buses in cities. Long-haul trucking unions are among India's most organised labour segments.
occupations.ai_rationale: ERP and route AI adopted by logistics firms (Delhivery, Blue Dart). Autonomous trucking on India's roads is not viable for 15+ years.

occupations.name: Factory & Industrial Machine Operators
occupations.india_note: PLI scheme attracting mobile phone, auto components, pharma manufacturing to India. Foxconn, Samsung, Apple vendors creating formal machine operator demand. ITI-trained operators in demand — 15,000 ITIs train 15L students/year.
occupations.ai_rationale: Process automation and predictive maintenance AI transforming organised plants. New investment in India means newer, more automated facilities — fewer operators per unit output.

occupations.name: Domestic Workers (Maids, Cooks, Cleaners)
occupations.india_note: 2.5 crore domestic workers — 93% women, nearly all informal. No dedicated law despite decades of campaigns. Average wage ₹5,000–8,000/month in metros. Urban Company partially formalising skilled segment (housekeeping, deep cleaning).
occupations.ai_rationale: Domestic work is entirely physical in variable household environments — cleaning, cooking, and childcare remain irreplaceable human services.

occupations.name: Agricultural Labourers (Casual/Daily Wage)
occupations.india_note: 9 crore landless labourers — India's most economically vulnerable. MNREGA guarantees 100 days/year employment at ₹237–374/day by state. National average MNREGA wage: ₹267/day (2024). Women are 57% of MNREGA workforce.
occupations.ai_rationale: Farm labouring — weeding, harvesting, transplanting — is entirely manual. Even where mechanisation is possible (combine harvesters), the economics often favour labour at India's wage levels.

occupations.name: Construction Labourers (Daily Wage)
occupations.india_note: 3.8 crore construction workers, 80% migrant. Building & Other Construction Workers Act 1996 provides welfare fund — ₹75,000+ crore collected, only 20% disbursed. e-Shram registered 28 crore unorganised workers by 2024.
occupations.ai_rationale: Load bearing, digging, brick-laying — physical and unstructured. Construction robots are experimental globally; economically infeasible in India's labour market for decades.

occupations.name: Sanitation & Waste Collection Workers
occupations.india_note: Swachh Bharat Mission created formal sanitation workforce in municipalities. SC/manual scavengers — 58,000 identified (official), likely far more. SC/ST Welfare allocations increasing sanitation worker wages. Mechanised sewer cleaning mandate reducing manual hazards.
occupations.ai_rationale: Waste collection and sanitation are physical municipal services. Mechanised sweepers reduce headcount in organised municipalities but informal waste pickers remain.

occupations.name: Delivery & Logistics Workers
occupations.india_note: India Post, BlueDart, Delhivery, Ecom Express, DTDC employ millions. E-commerce delivery growing 28% YoY. Average last-mile delivery salary ₹8,000–12,000/month. Often classified as contractors to avoid PF/ESIC obligations.
occupations.ai_rationale: Route AI fully deployed by all major logistics firms. Last-mile delivery in India's unaddressed lanes, gated societies, and varied building types still requires human judgment.
